model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With warmup+cosine already in place and only 256 steps, a higher peak learning rate can improve early progress and reach lower validation loss within the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), total_steps // 2)","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), total_steps // 2)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Shortening warmup from 10% to 5% lets the model spend more of the 256-step budget near the stronger learning-rate regime, which can improve fixed-budget convergence.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), total_steps // 2)","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), total_steps // 2)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The previous warmup-shortening regressed validation loss, so restoring the earlier 10% warmup is the highest-confidence next step to recover better fixed-budget convergence.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP width modestly to improve representation capacity while keeping architecture simple and compatible with the same fixed-step training budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Continue the successful width-scaling trend with a small additional capacity increase to improve fit under the same optimization settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The extra width slightly regressed loss, so reverting to the previously better-capacity setting restores the stronger baseline before exploring other changes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed 256 steps, increasing batch size raises unique-example throughput and can improve validation loss by covering more of the dataset within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given the strong gain from larger batches under fixed steps, testing a further increase can improve sample coverage and gradient stability within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has consistently improved fixed-step performance; testing 1024 may further increase data coverage and reduce gradient noise within the same 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The fixed-step regime continues to reward larger batches; moving to 2048 should further increase effective epoch coverage and may reduce validation loss within 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After large-batch scaling, a moderately higher learning rate is a natural follow-up to improve optimization speed and potentially reduce validation loss further within the same 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With very high effective epoch coverage from large batches and fixed steps, slightly stronger weight decay may improve generalization and lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), total_steps // 2)","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 5, 1), total_steps // 2)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"At larger batch and higher learning rate, a longer warmup can stabilize early optimization and improve final validation loss under the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        start_factor=0.2,","new":"        start_factor=0.1,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With longer warmup and high peak LR, lowering the warmup start factor can reduce early-step instability and improve final validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After stabilization improvements, a small LR increase may improve progress during the cosine phase without changing overall schedule structure.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the strongest signal so far; increasing to 4096 is a direct follow-up to test whether even lower gradient noise and larger sample coverage per step further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With very large-batch training now dominant, a modest LR increase is a low-complexity way to improve optimization progress per step without increasing runtime pressure further.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Now that optimization is much stronger, a modest width increase may unlock additional capacity that was previously undertrained and further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With larger model capacity and very large-batch training now working well, a final modest LR increase is the most direct low-complexity attempt to improve fixed-step convergence further.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}
